doc: Per-package statement-coverage FLOOR for the local-compile pipeline (plan-5 step 8). Each value is the minimum acceptable `go test -coverprofile` total for that package; TestCoverageFloor reds if a package drops below its floor. Floors are frozen a couple points BELOW the measured coverage at authoring (2026-06-19) so the gate is real but not flaky from a single dropped line. internal/model has no executable statements (pure type/json-tag declarations), so its profile reports `[no statements]`; the runner treats a no-statement package as a vacuous pass and its floor is 0. RE-BASELINE: after an INTENTIONAL coverage change, measure `go test -cover ./internal/<pkg>/` for each package, set each floor a couple points below the new value, and commit this file with the diff. See docs/spec/compiler/conformance-manifest-schema.md (Coverage floor).
